title: Fulbright Specialist Program
description: A U.S. Department of State program (implemented by World Learning) that sends established U.S. faculty and professionals abroad for 2-6 week, project-based assignments at host institutions -- universities, NGOs, think tanks, ministries -- covering curriculum development, institutional planning, and technical consulting across a wide range of fields. Applicants must be U.S. citizens with significant professional or academic experience (roughly five years minimum); once accepted onto the three-year roster, they are matched to specific projects proposed by host institutions in any of roughly 150 participating countries and areas, including Mongolia. Funding covers round-trip economy airfare, a modest transit allowance, limited health-benefit coverage, and a $200/day honorarium, with host institutions providing or contributing to in-country lodging, meals, and local transport.
org: U.S. Department of State / Fulbright Program (World Learning)
visaNote: Specialists travel on the visa type required by the host country for short-term technical/academic visits; the host institution and local Fulbright Commission or U.S. Embassy assist with arrangements, which vary by destination.

title: RIAS Berlin Commission Journalist Program
description: A binational German-American journalist exchange run by the RIAS Berlin Commission, supported through Germany's transatlantic program funding, that sends active U.S. broadcast/online journalists to Germany (with a stop in Brussels) for an intensive two-week study tour of newsrooms, political institutions, and media organizations. Applicants must be full-time or freelance radio, TV, or online journalists with roughly five years of professional experience; German language skills are not required. The program covers round-trip airfare, in-country travel, and hotel accommodations, with most meals also provided.
visaNote: U.S. citizens can enter Germany visa-free for this short professional visit under the Schengen 90-day visa-waiver arrangement; no separate work visa is required.
